description: Finding templates (English-first). One sentence risk + one sentence impact + 3-item approval checklist. Future: add i18n_key, keep English as fallback.

finding_templates.title: Destructive filesystem (blocked)
finding_templates.risk_summary: Commands like rm -rf /, mkfs, wipefs can destroy runner, workspace, or production data.
finding_templates.impact_summary: Unbounded destructive operations may cause irreversible data loss.
finding_templates.remediation_tpl: Confirm paths are allowlisted; use isolated env for destructive ops.
approval_checklist: Confirm target paths are explicit and allowlisted (no wildcards on /).
Run destructive operations only in isolated env (ephemeral runner, sandbox).

finding_templates.title: Privilege/SSH tampering (blocked)
finding_templates.risk_summary: Plaintext or long-lived private keys, sshd_config, authorized_keys increase credential and persistence risk.
finding_templates.impact_summary: SSH/key tampering can enable lateral movement or persistent access.
finding_templates.remediation_tpl: Use short-lived credentials, secret manager, or read-only deploy keys.
approval_checklist: Use short-lived credentials or secret manager; avoid long-lived private keys in skill.
Prefer read-only deploy keys; do not modify sshd_config or authorized_keys from skill.

finding_templates.title: Remote pipe execution (blocked)
finding_templates.risk_summary: curl|wget ... | bash/sh is treated as a hard block by many orgs (supply-chain/RCE risk).
finding_templates.impact_summary: Remote content executed without integrity check can compromise the system.
finding_templates.remediation_tpl: Download to file, verify checksum/signature, then execute; or use a trusted install path.
approval_checklist: Remove remote pipe pattern (curl|wget ... | bash/sh).
Use download-then-verify-then-run with fixed artifact.
Re-run scan and re-attempt release.

finding_templates.title: OffSec content detected (forced classification override)
finding_templates.risk_summary: Content matches offensive security patterns and is treated as OffSec.
finding_templates.impact_summary: In General profile, OffSec distribution is blocked by policy.
finding_templates.remediation_tpl: If this is for a sanctioned security exercise, move to RedTeam repo/profile with stricter RBAC and audit.
approval_checklist: Is this actually for a sanctioned security exercise?
If yes, move to RedTeam repo/profile with stricter RBAC and audit.
Confirm the skill is not accessible to general production users.

finding_templates.title: Requires elevated privileges (sudo/admin)
finding_templates.risk_summary: Running as admin increases blast radius for mistakes and injection.
finding_templates.impact_summary: A bad command can modify the system globally or leak sensitive data.
finding_templates.remediation_tpl: Limit scope (hosts/env), use least privilege, document and test rollback.
approval_checklist: Is the target scope (hosts/env) explicitly limited?
Are commands/args fixed or can user input reach the shell?
Is rollback documented (and tested) for all changes?

finding_templates.title: Controls system services (start/stop/restart/reload)
finding_templates.risk_summary: Service control can cause downtime or partial outages.
finding_templates.impact_summary: Restart/reload errors can take production traffic down.
finding_templates.remediation_tpl: Restrict to maintenance windows or non-prod; add health-check and automatic rollback on failure.
approval_checklist: Is it restricted to maintenance windows or non-prod?
Is there a health-check and automatic rollback on failure?
Is the affected service list explicit and minimal?

finding_templates.title: Writes to system directories
finding_templates.risk_summary: Modifying system directories can create persistence or break OS-level behavior.
finding_templates.impact_summary: System integrity and security boundaries may be compromised.
finding_templates.remediation_tpl: Provide exact paths and file diffs; document backup and rollback; consider writing under an application directory instead.
approval_checklist: Exact paths + file diffs provided?
Backup + rollback steps included?
Can changes be scoped to app directories instead?

finding_templates.title: Changes file permissions/ownership
finding_templates.risk_summary: Permission changes can unintentionally grant broader access.
finding_templates.impact_summary: May enable privilege escalation or data exposure.
finding_templates.remediation_tpl: List before/after permissions; apply only to required files/dirs (no recursive wildcards); provide rollback script.
approval_checklist: Before/after permissions listed?
Applies only to required files/dirs (not recursive wildcards)?
Is there a rollback script to restore permissions?

finding_templates.title: Downloads and executes remote content
finding_templates.risk_summary: Remote download + immediate execution increases supply-chain and RCE risk.
finding_templates.impact_summary: A compromised download source can lead to full system compromise.
finding_templates.remediation_tpl: Pin artifacts (checksum/signature), use a trusted mirror, and separate download/verify/execute steps.
approval_checklist: Is the download source trusted and allowlisted (domain/repo)?
Is integrity verified (SHA256/signature) before execution?
Is there a rollback plan if installation/upgrade breaks?

finding_templates.title: Skill file set exceeds limit
finding_templates.risk_summary: Single skill file count or total size exceeds governance limit.
finding_templates.impact_summary: Governance and audit cost is high; may indicate unbounded scope.
finding_templates.remediation_tpl: Reduce to under 2000 files and 50MB; or split into multiple skills.
approval_checklist: Is the scope justified and documented?
Can the skill be split into smaller units?
Is there an inventory of what is included?

finding_templates.title: Nested skill detected
finding_templates.risk_summary: Nested skill directories make governance boundaries unclear.
finding_templates.impact_summary: Approval and catalog scope may be ambiguous.
finding_templates.remediation_tpl: Flatten directory structure or document nested scope explicitly.
approval_checklist: Is the nested scope documented?
Is it clear which paths belong to which skill?
Can the structure be flattened?

finding_templates.title: Anchor outside skills/ directory
finding_templates.risk_summary: Skill anchor is not under the convention directory skills/**.
finding_templates.impact_summary: Discovery and governance are harder; repo convention is broken.
finding_templates.remediation_tpl: Place all skill anchors under skills/** for consistent discovery.
approval_checklist: Is there a reason to keep skills outside skills/?
Can anchors be moved under skills/?
Is the repo convention documented for the team?
